soap:
RE-REVIEW · lancedb/lancedb#3303 — feat: index analyze support
reviewed b73b7310 → badb3679  (10 commits, 12 files)
domain: rust/lancedb (index analysis + new datafusion batch-KNN operator)

================================================================
SUBJECTIVE
================================================================
Since my last review the author replaced the ground-truth (GT) phase of
`analyze_index` — previously a per-query `nearest_to` loop with a
`// TODO: swap for Lance's batch KNN primitive` placeholder — with a brand-new,
purpose-built brute-force batch-KNN DataFusion operator (`table/datafusion/knn.rs`
+ `knn_ext.rs` + `knn_optimizer.rs`, public wrapper `table/knn.rs`, and a new
integration test `tests/knn_test.rs`). The three prior comments I left were all
non-blocking follow-up notes (two `suggestion`, one `non_blocking`), none gated
the PR. This re-review therefore turns mostly on a fresh-eyes pass over the new
~1100 lines of operator code, where the delta concentrates.

================================================================
OBJECTIVE
================================================================
Delta scoped to PR's own files (0 off-branch exclusions). Files of substance:
  rust/lancedb/src/index/analyze.rs           +142 -56  (GT phase rewrite)
  rust/lancedb/src/table/datafusion/knn.rs    +818      (new operator, core)
  rust/lancedb/src/table/datafusion/knn_ext.rs +103     (DataFrame .knn() ext)
  rust/lancedb/src/table/datafusion/knn_optimizer.rs +79 (vector-projection rule)
  rust/lancedb/src/table/knn.rs               +56       (public batch_knn())
  rust/lancedb/tests/knn_test.rs              +279      (new integration tests)
  rust/lancedb/src/table/datafusion.rs        +17       (BaseTableAdapter.with_row_id)
  + Cargo.{toml,lock}, docs.

================================================================
ASSESSMENT
================================================================

----------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION A — CONDITIONS LEDGER (0 blocking; 0/3 closed-ended "met")
----------------------------------------------------------------
None of my three prior comments were blocking. Status verified against the
badb3679 diff, not GitHub's resolved flags.

~ moot   [non_blocking] "support u8 (binary) vectors with hamming distance"
         (analyze.rs old line ~551)
         → The author took a different, explicit approach. At head, that region is
           now `vector_values_to_f32` (analyze_full.rs:551-573), which *guards*:
           non-float element types return a clear `InvalidInput` ("supports
           Float16/Float32/Float64"), rather than silently mis-handling u8. The
           original comment itself said "can be done as a follow up"; the GT path
           is now correctly bounded rather than wrong. Treated as moot for this PR.
           NOTE: the *new* knn operator separately DOES handle Hamming over u8
           (tests/knn_test.rs::test_hamming_binary_vectors), but analyze's GT
           sampler restricts itself to float columns by design.

✗ not_met [suggestion] "stream results out as they come in"
         (analyze.rs old line ~323)
         → The streaming-out suggestion was explicitly "can be done in the future".
           The GT phase was rewritten (now collects the whole batch_knn DataFrame
           via `df.collect()`, analyze_full.rs ~471-487) — still materialized, not
           streamed. Not addressed; remains a future item. Non-blocking by its own
           framing.

✗ not_met [suggestion] "make the remote analyze an IPC stream, not an IPC file …
           should be done as part of this PR since hard to change later"
         (rust/lancedb/src/remote/table.rs:1967)
         → remote/table.rs is NOT in the delta (confirmed: absent from the
           b73b7310...badb3679 compare). The author has not touched it since my
           review, so this cannot be "met". At head it still uses `FileReader`
           (IPC file; remote/table.rs:42) with the standing comment "There isn't a
           way to actually stream this data yet … upstream issue" (line 427).
           This is the one I framed as "should be done as part of this PR" — worth
           re-raising with the attending, but I left it as a suggestion, not a
           blocking token, so it does not by itself gate.

----------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION B — FRESH-EYES DELTA (new code since b73b7310)
----------------------------------------------------------------
The new batch-KNN operator is the bulk of the delta and is where the risk lives.
One genuine, blocking correctness bug; two lower-severity robustness items.

🔴 issue(blocking): top-k heap orders distances by raw f32 *bit* pattern, which
   inverts for negative distances → wrong neighbors for Dot / Cosine at k≥2.
   rust/lancedb/src/table/datafusion/knn.rs:308-343, 552-556
   `HeapEntry::Ord` compares `dist.to_bits()` as a plain `u32`
   (`self.dist_bits.cmp(&other.dist_bits)`, line 341). The doc comment itself
   concedes this only holds "for positive values" (lines 305-310). For NEGATIVE
   f32, the sign bit makes the bit pattern sort *larger*, so u32 ordering is
   reversed vs the float. In the per-partition eviction loop the admission test
   uses correct f32 comparison (`dist < heap.peek().dist()`, line 552) but the
   `heap.pop()` that evicts the worst entry (line 555) relies on the broken `Ord`.
   When distances can be negative, `pop()` discards the *nearest* candidate
   instead of the farthest, corrupting the kept top-k.
   Lance's Dot distance is negative for positively-correlated vectors, and Cosine
   can be slightly negative; both feed in via `lance_dist.arrow_batch_func()`
   (line 512). L2 and Hamming are always ≥0 and are unaffected.
   Why it slipped tests: cosine is only tested at k=1 (test_cosine_distance),
   where the heap never exceeds 1 and `pop()` is never exercised; the only k≥2
   test uses L2. `analyze_index` sweeps multiple k values, so a Cosine- or
   Dot-typed index would hit this in the GT phase and silently report wrong recall.
   Fix: order the heap on the actual f32 (e.g. `total_cmp`, or normalize bits with
   the standard sign-flip trick), and add a k≥2 Dot/Cosine test.

🟡 issue (non-blocking): MergeKnnExec infers the query count from the data, not the
   plan — a query with zero surviving neighbors is silently dropped.
   rust/lancedb/src/table/datafusion/knn.rs:775-781
   `num_queries = max_qi + 1` from observed `_query_index` values. A query index
   that produced no rows in any partition (empty input, or all its rows lost) never
   appears, shrinks num_queries, and is dropped with no error. In practice every
   query is compared against all rows so each gets ≥1 neighbor on a non-empty
   table — edge case only. Prefer carrying `query_vectors.len()` through the plan.

🟡 issue (non-blocking): `k == 0` panics; general-purpose primitive should validate k.
   rust/lancedb/src/table/datafusion/knn.rs:552
   With k=0, `heap.len() < k` is false, so the first row evaluates
   `heap.peek().unwrap()` on an empty heap → panic. analyze_index always uses k≥1,
   but `batch_knn`/`DataFrame::knn` are public; validate k≥1 at construction.

⚪ nit (non-blocking): doc/code drift on the output column name.
   knn_ext.rs:30-34 documents the added column as `query_index: Int32`, but the
   operator emits `_query_index` (knn.rs:353-357; consumers in analyze.rs and
   tests read `_query_index`). Doc only.

Everything else in the new operator checked out on a careful read: multi-partition
merge regroups globally (no split/dup), global→(batch,local) index mapping via
`partition_point` is correct, `with_row_id` schema plumbing is sound, and the
optimizer's vector-projection rule fails safe (keeps the vector when unsure).

================================================================
PLAN / RECOMMENDATION
================================================================
RECOMMENDATION: block on 1 condition (the negative-distance heap-ordering bug).
  The prior ledger carries nothing blocking, but the fresh-eyes pass found a real
  correctness regression in code added since my last review: top-k is silently
  wrong for Dot/Cosine indexes at k≥2 — exactly what analyze_index's GT phase
  exercises — so analyze would report bogus recall for those index types.

  confidence: high on the bug (the Ord impl's own doc admits the positive-only
  precondition; eviction path uses it; no k≥2 negative-distance test exists).
  what would make this wrong: if Lance's `arrow_batch_func` for Dot/Cosine is
  guaranteed to return only non-negative values here (it is not — Dot distance is
  routinely negative), the heap ordering would be fine and this drops to a nit.

  Re-raise (non-blocking) with the attending: the remote IPC-stream suggestion I
  flagged as "do it in this PR" was not addressed and remote/table.rs is untouched.
